title: belgian potatoes vlam fhm2025
full_text: Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Now Reading: Belgian Potatoes Proudly Takes Center Stage In Malaysia 1 01 Belgian Potatoes Proudly Takes Center Stage In Malaysia Main// Eat// Drink// Reviews// Insider// At Home// About Us// Download Our Magazine// Q2 2026// Q1 2026// Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026// Home Eat Belgian Potatoes Proudly Takes Center Stage In MalaysiaBelgian Potatoes Proudly Takes Center Stage In MalaysiaPatrick LohEat8 months agoShare Belgium’s golden export has found a loyal following in Malaysia. At Food & Hospitality Malaysia 2025, VLAM brought five of the country’s finest potato producers to showcase why Belgian frozen potatoes are winning hearts, kitchens, and appetites across the nation. When the golden crisp of a perfect fry hits your plate, it rarely comes with the story behind it. Yet at Food and Hospitality Malaysia 2025 (FHM 2025), Belgium made sure that every texture, every cut, and every crunch carried not just flavour, but heritage, precision, and craft. Under VLAM’s banner, five leading exporters—Agristo, Clarebout Potatoes, Ecofrost, Global Fries, and Pomuni—revealed why Malaysia has become one of Asia’s most enthusiastic fans of Belgian frozen potato products. A nation’s appetite, a bean of demand Malaysia is no stranger to fries, whether it’s curly, crinkle, shoestring, or steak-cut. But behind the craving lies a robust import story. In 2024 alone, Malaysia imported over 17,000 tonnes of processed potato products from Belgium, valued at more than RM109 million. The momentum didn’t stop there: the first few months of 2025 saw an additional 5,800 tonnes shipped in, worth some RM41 million. VLAM’s showcase at FHM 2025 was more than just display windows of frozen trays. It was a conversation with flavors, efficiency, and evolving expectations. For restaurateurs, hotel chefs, quick-service operators, and distributors, the allure wasn’t just ‘Belgian fries.’ It was consistent quality, format variety, and supply reliability. Meet the potato artisans Each of the five brands brings its own signature behind the crisp: Agristo: Known for its wide variety of fries, from thin cuts to crinkle fries, wedges to mini hash browns. It melds versatility with dependable flavour and it’s ready to adapt to local needs. Clarebout Potatoes: Mydibel among its associations, Clarebout is steeped in family heritage. Their offerings privilege texture and form is a golden hue, a tender inside, and a satisfying crunch outside. Ecofrost: Precision meets innovation here. Ecofrost stands out with strong certifications (BRC, IFS) and a focus on food safety. Their potato flakes, mashed bases, and variety-specialty cuts allow chefs to play, be it mash or crisp fry. Global Fries: A name that suggests both reach and specialization. Known for its operational flexibility and ability to supply large volume while keeping consistency. Pomuni: Perhaps less well-known in broader discussions, but very present among the five exporters at the showcase. Pomuni’s frozen potato offerings bring contributions to variety and product format breadth. Why Malaysia listens Delicious fried potato on wooden plate with dipped sauce – traditional fast food concept What makes this moment so pivotal? Perhaps it’s this mix of quality and need. Malaysia’s foodservice sector is growing fast. Urbanisation, more dining-out habits, a rising demand for comfort food done well. Premium frozen potato products hit a sweet spot: they’re familiar, beloved, and efficient. Belgian potatoes offer a menu of cuts: shoestring, crinkle, steak fries, wedges, hash browns. This cut variety isn’t simply aesthetic but functional. Different cuts thrive in different contexts. For instance, quick-service joints want something fast- frying; fine dining might want steak cuts or specialty potato forms as garnishes. The Belgian exporters showcased that they can deliver across the board. Sustainability also gets a mention. Not just as nice-to-have, but increasingly expected. European producers, including these Belgian potato exporters, are leaning into eco-conscientious production, from traceability to packaging and energy use. Malaysia’s chefs and industry players are taking note. Beyond fries: the subtle innovations While fries continue to win hearts, the showcase revealed more nuanced tools in the potato toolbox. Croquettes, mash, tater tots, hash browns: formats that allow menus to stretch without sacrificing flavour or consistency. Ideas like smaller-format portions, specialty seasoning (less oil, better oil), or formats suitable for ovens or air-fryers are gaining traction. This flexibility is part of what makes Belgian exports more than just frozen—it is partly innovation. For chefs, reliability is gold. Knowing that your supplier offers consistency across batches means that whether you’re frying in Kuala Lumpur or whipping up mash in Penang, the final bite meets diners’ expectations. That is one of the core selling points. The final bite As we left the Belgian pavilion at FHM 2025, there was a quiet satisfaction in the air, a kind of hunger satisfied, but also expectation raised. These five exporters—Agristo, Clarebout Potatoes, Ecofrost, Global Fries, Pomuni—aren’t simply here to supply fries. They’re part of a growing movement in Malaysia’s food industry: one that values the golden crunch, yes, but also values provenance, innovation, and sustainable quality. The story of frozen potatoes is not just about convenience. It’s a narrative of trust between chef and supplier, between local taste demands and global craftsmanship, between the expectations of crunchy exteriors and tender hearts. Malaysia’s kitchens are evolving. And Belgian frozen potatoes, under the spotlight of VLAM’s campaign, are helping lead that evolution. So the next time you order a plate of fries, a loaded hash brown, or a crisp croquette, take a moment to appreciate the journey. From Flemish fields to Malaysian freezers, every golden bite has its backstory—and now, thanks to this showcase, more Malaysians know just how rich it can be.
